findings.evidence: `rg -n "toNormalizerOfNormal" code/TauCeti -g '*.lean'` finds only lines 111, 118, 124, and the inverse representative lemma at 186 in this new file.
findings.fix: Inline this construction in `normalizerQuotientEquivQuotientOfNormal_symm_mk` or keep only a private helper; expose only quotient/equivalence lemmas needed by consumers.
findings.issue: `toNormalizerOfNormal` and `toNormalizerOfNormal_surjective` are public helper API for putting `G` into its normalizer under `[H.Normal]`, not part of the named `N(H)/H` surface.

findings.evidence: Mathlib provides `QuotientGroup.mk'_surjective` and `[simp] theorem range_mk'` in `mathlib/Mathlib/GroupTheory/QuotientGroup/Defs.lean:99` and `:131`, while `rg -n "normalizerQuotientMk.*surjective|normalizerQuotientMk.*range" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotient.lean` has no hits.
findings.fix: Add `normalizerQuotientMk_surjective` and preferably a `[simp]` `normalizerQuotientMk_range` lemma, restating the existing `QuotientGroup` facts for this API.
findings.issue: The public quotient map has no named surjectivity/range lemma, so users of the stable wrapper must unfold to `QuotientGroup.mk'_surjective`/`range_mk'` to eliminate arbitrary elements of `normalizerQuotient H`.

summary: The quotient wrapper exposes avoidable normal-case scaffolding and is missing a named representative/surjectivity fact for the canonical quotient map, so downstream users still have to lean on the underlying `QuotientGroup` implementation.
